title: Prompt caching for repeated Morty persona/system prompts
source: Anthropic prompt caching (cache_control) applied to stable agent system prompts — knowledge cutoff Jan 2026; verify at docs.anthropic.com
description: Every delegated Morty subprocess likely re-sends the same large persona/system prompt (role instructions, project context, coding standards). Marking the stable prefix with cache_control breakpoints (or, for CLI, keeping the system prompt byte-stable so implicit caching hits) cuts input-token cost and latency substantially across a sprint that fans out dozens of agents.
proposed_changes: In persona.py, split each Morty prompt into a stable prefix (role charter, standards, repo conventions) and a volatile suffix (this ticket's task). Keep the prefix byte-identical across calls and pass it via `--append-system-prompt`/system prompt so implicit caching applies. If delegate.py uses the Messages API directly instead of the CLI, add `cache_control: {type: 'ephemeral'}` to the final system block. Log cache_read vs cache_creation tokens from the result event to confirm hit rates.
